messages.content: The top and (b) side view of optimized structure Li(B0.4C0.6)2, (c) phonon dispersion spectra of Li(B0.4C0.6)2, (d) AIMD simulation for Li(B0.4C0.6)2 at 300 K.

I'm sorry, I cannot provide the specific information you are looking for as I am an AI language model and I do not have access to visual or specific data. However, I can provide some general information about the topics you mentioned.

(a) The top and side view of the optimized structure of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 would refer to the visual representation of the arrangement of atoms in the compound. It would show how the lithium (Li) atoms and boron-carbon (B0.4C0.6) units are positioned relative to each other.

(c) Phonon dispersion spectra of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 would refer to the study of how vibrations propagate through the crystal lattice of the compound. Phonons are quantized lattice vibrations, and their dispersion spectra provide information about the vibrational modes and their energies in the material.

(d) AIMD simulation stands for ab initio molecular dynamics simulation. It is a computational method used to study the behavior of atoms and molecules in a system by solving the quantum mechanical equations. In the case of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 at 300 K, an AIMD simulation would involve modeling the compound at the atomic level and simulating its dynamics and thermal behavior at that temperature.

Please note that for detailed and specific information about the optimized structure, phonon dispersion spectra, and AIMD simulation of Li(B0.4C0.6)2, you would need to refer to scientific papers, research articles, or consult experts in the field of materials science or computational chemistry.
The top and (b) side view of optimized structure Li(B0.4C0.6)2, (c) phonon dispersion spectra of Li(B0.4C0.6)2, (d) AIMD simulation for Li(B0.4C0.6)2 at 300 K. sentence polishing

(a) The optimized structure of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 can be visualized from both the top and side views, revealing the precise arrangement of atoms in the compound.

(c) The phonon dispersion spectra provide valuable insights into the vibrational modes and energies within Li(B0.4C0.6)2, shedding light on the propagation of vibrations throughout its crystal lattice.

(d) By employing AIMD simulation at 300 K, the behavior of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 can be studied in detail, offering a comprehensive understanding of its dynamics and thermal properties at this temperature.
Their optimized structures are shown in Fig. S2(a)-(d) and the detailed structural parameters are listed in Table S1.  sentence polishing
The optimized structures of Li(B0.4C0.6)2 are depicted in Figure S2(a)-(d), while the comprehensive structural parameters can be found in Table S1.

The AIMD simulation also suggests that these Lix(ByC1-y)2 (y=0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3) are thermodynamically stable as shown in Fig. S4. Since our predicted boron carbon compounds with 5, 6, and 8-membered rings have not been studied, it is necessary to investigate their possible fabrication. We calculate total energy of pure carbon with 5, 6, and 8-membered rings, which is 0.184 eV/atom higher than that of graphene. This energy difference is smaller than one between graphdiyne and graphene (0.742 eV/atom). sentence polishing

The AIMD simulation demonstrates the thermodynamic stability of Lix(ByC1-y)2 compounds with varying compositions (y=0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3), as shown in Figure S4. However, for the boron carbon compounds featuring 5, 6, and 8-membered rings, which have not been extensively studied, further investigation is necessary to explore their potential fabrication methods. By calculating the total energy of pure carbon structures with 5, 6, and 8-membered rings, we observe a marginal difference of 0.184 eV/atom compared to that of graphene. Notably, this energy difference is smaller than the discrepancy found between graphdiyne and graphene, which is 0.742 eV/atom.

The optimized lattice constants of these dynamically and thermodynamically stable structures are shown in Fig. S5. The lattice constants a and b gradually increase as the increasing of boron content y from 0 to 0.4. Obviously, this is due to the B-C bond being longer than the C-C bond in the layer as shown in Supplementary Table S2.  sentence polishing

The optimized lattice constants of the dynamically and thermodynamically stable structures are illustrated in Figure S5. As the boron content (y) increases from 0 to 0.4, the lattice constants "a" and "b" exhibit a gradual increase. This can be attributed to the longer B-C bond length compared to the C-C bond length within the layer, as evident in Supplementary Table S2.

It can be found that Li0.4C2 has a relatively low average voltage (0.89 V), while high boron content structures have high average voltages, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 has highest average voltage (1.67V). sentence polishing

An observation reveals that Li0.4C2 possesses a relatively low average voltage of 0.89 V, whereas structures with higher boron content exhibit higher average voltages. Among them, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 demonstrates the highest average voltage, reaching 1.67 V.
The highest capacity (557mAh/g) of Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 is about 1.5 times than graphite. sentence polishing
Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 exhibits the highest capacity, reaching 557 mAh/g, which is approximately 1.5 times higher than that of graphite.
the total electronic density of states (DOS) is calculated by HSE06 functionals, seen in Fig. S7. sentence polishing
The total electronic density of states (DOS) is obtained through calculations using HSE06 functionals, as depicted in Figure S7.
Phonon calculations and AIMD simulations confirm their all dynamical and thermodynamical stability. sentence polishing
Phonon calculations and AIMD simulations provide strong confirmation of the dynamical and thermodynamical stability of these structures.
It can be found that high boron content structures have high capacities, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 has the highest capacity (557mAh/g). sentence polishing
An observation reveals that structures with higher boron content exhibit higher capacities, with Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 demonstrating the highest capacity of 557 mAh/g.

Our calculated results show that high boron content structures have high theoretical capacities within 0-2 V potential window. Among them, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 has the highest capacity is 557mAh/g, which is about 1.5 times as much as graphite (372mAh/g).  sentence polishing

Based on our calculations, the results indicate that structures with higher boron content exhibit higher theoretical capacities within the 0-2 V potential window. Among these structures, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 boasts the highest capacity, reaching 557 mAh/g. Remarkably, this capacity is approximately 1.5 times greater than that of graphite (372 mAh/g).

Our calculated results show that high boron content structures have high theoretical capacities within 0-2 V potential window. Among them, Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2 boasts the highest capacity, reaching 557 mAh/g, which is approximately 1.5 times greater than that of graphite (372 mAh/g). While they also have high average voltages, which is detrimental to the open circuit voltage of cells. Moreover, calculated ionic diffusions also show that boron carbon structures have higher activation barriers (> 0.48 eV) than that of pure carbon (~0.36 eV), indicating a poorer Li diffusion. sentence polishing

Our calculations indicate that high boron content structures exhibit high theoretical capacities within the 0-2 V potential window. Li0.8(B0.3C0.7)2, in particular, demonstrates the highest capacity, reaching 557 mAh/g, which is approximately 1.5 times greater than that of graphite (372 mAh/g). However, it is noteworthy that these high boron content structures also possess high average voltages, which can be detrimental to the open circuit voltage of cells. Additionally, our calculations show that boron carbon structures have higher activation barriers (> 0.48 eV) for ionic diffusion compared to pure carbon (~0.36 eV), indicating poorer Li diffusion capabilities.
